title: hibiscus calyculatus
intro: Hibiscus calyculatus, also known as the calyculous hibiscus or yellow hibiscus, is a species of flowering plant in the mallow family, native to Australia and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of hibiscus calyculatus include lowering blood pressure, reducing cholesterol levels, and acting as a natural diuretic, making it a popular remedy for cardiovascular issues and urinary problems. Hibiscus calyculatus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal supplements and topical applications, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically proven.
